name: Roasted Salsa Spareribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a good charcoal fire in the grill. When the coals are almost gray and create an even layer.
2. Set tomatoes and chiles on lightly greased grill, 4-6 inches above the coals. Cook, turning as needed, until the chiles are charred on all sides and the tomatoes are hot and streaked with brown.
3. Add a small amount of new charcoal and mound for indirect heat.
4. To make salsa, chop grilled tomatoes and chiles; discard stems and seeds. Place tomatoes and chiles and their juices in a bowl. Stir in vinegar, cilantro, and garlic; season to taste with salt.
5. Spread some of the salsa evenly over both sides of ribs.
6. Place ribs, meat side up, on grill directly above the drip pan (overlap ribs if necessary). Cover the barbecue and adjust dampers as necessary to maintain an even heat.
7. Cook, basting occasionally with some of the remaining salsa, until the meat near the bone is no longer pink; test by cutting (1-1 1/2 hours).
8. To serve, cut ribs into 2 to 3 rib portions. Garnish with lime slices if desired. Pass remaining salsa, sour cream, and lime wedges at the table.
9. Eat by topping ribs with salsa, sour cream, and a squeeze of lime.
